Politics.headline: Trump says Iran conflict is 'terminated' as he hits congressional deadline
Politics.detail: President Trump has stated that hostilities with Iran have ended, coinciding with a deadline for congressional approval of the conflict, though he didn't provide further details on the conditions or long-term implications.

Politics.headline: Asylum seeker sent back to France in 'one in, one out' scheme to be returned to Syria
Politics.detail: An asylum seeker who was initially sent back to France under a 'one in, one out' scheme is now set to be returned to Syria, raising concerns about the safety and ethical implications of such policies.

Politics.headline: Trump expands red snapper fishing as critics warn of overfishing
Politics.detail: President Trump has broadened red snapper fishing regulations, sparking criticism from environmentalists who caution against potential overfishing and ecological damage.

Politics.headline: U.S. blockade has cost Iran $4.8 billion, Pentagon says
Politics.detail: The U.S. Department of Defense estimates that the U.S. naval blockade in the Gulf of Oman has cost Iran approximately $4.8 billion in oil revenue, increasing pressure on the Iranian government during ongoing peace talks.

Politics.headline: Trump says he's not satisfied with Iran's proposal to end the war
Politics.detail: President Trump has expressed his dissatisfaction with Iran's latest proposal to end the war, even as diplomatic efforts continue amidst a U.S. naval blockade and considerations for renewed military actions.

Finance & Economy.headline: The world's central banks are wrestling with a gigantic problem
Finance & Economy.detail: Central bankers in Tokyo, Washington, London, and Frankfurt are hesitant to shift interest rates amidst surging inflation, slowing economic growth, and the energy shock caused by the war in Iran. Federal Reserve policymakers decided to hold rates steady due to the uncertainty in the Middle East, marking Jerome Powell's last meeting as chair.

Finance & Economy.headline: BPInsights: May 2, 2026
Finance & Economy.detail: Large banks have increased their holdings of Treasury securities to the highest level since the Global Financial Crisis, influenced by recent changes in capital rules. The Treasury Department froze $344 million in cryptocurrency in digital wallets tied to Iran, which has been demanding tolls to access the Strait of Hormuz be paid in cryptocurrency, a key means of sanctions evasion.

Finance & Economy.headline: Spirit ceases operations amid financial struggles and high oil prices
Finance & Economy.detail: Spirit Airlines announced it will cease operations after years of financial struggles and two bankruptcy filings since 2024. The airline's attempt to secure a federal bailout from the Trump administration failed, leading to the cancellation of all flights and termination of customer service.

Finance & Economy.headline: Trump's tariff-friendly White House celebrates these foreign imports
Finance & Economy.detail: The Trump White House is acknowledging a surge in imports, particularly those related to the artificial intelligence industry, despite its general preference for domestic goods. Artificial intelligence is reshaping trade patterns, with companies increasingly ordering components from abroad to build AI infrastructure.

Finance & Economy.headline: India invokes force majeure, major relief for war-hit companies
Finance & Economy.detail: Due to the ongoing Gulf War, India has invoked force majeure, providing relief to companies affected by the conflict. Additionally, India is in discussions with Nepal regarding ethanol exports and is exploring potential shipments to other SAARC nations.

Technology.headline: OpenAI Eyes AI Agent Phone, Kuo Says
Technology.detail: OpenAI may be exploring an AI agent smartphone, signaling a potential move beyond apps toward a more integrated, assistant-driven mobile experience.

Technology.headline: Apple appears to have discontinued its cheapest Mac mini
Technology.detail: The compact desktop Mac now starts at $799 and comes with 512GB of storage.

Technology.headline: Cybercriminals Are Now Coming After Freight Cargo. And They're Doing a Great Job.
Technology.detail: Cybercriminals are now impersonating legitimate businesses to hijack freight, steal high-value shipments, and reroute deliveries, causing a surge of what is called 'strategic cargo theft'.

Technology.headline: US military reaches deals with 7 tech companies to use their AI on classified systems
Technology.detail: The Pentagon says it has reached deals with seven tech companies to use their artificial intelligence in its classified computer networks, allowing the military to tap into AI-powered capabilities to help it fight wars.

Technology.headline: TSMC Hits Pause on ASML's Newest Lithography for A13 Process
Technology.detail: TSMC has officially hit pause on ASML's newest lithography technology, a move that has captured the attention of the entire semiconductor industry; the chipmaking giant decided against purchasing ASML's latest High-NA EUV machines for its upcoming process node, asserting that the capability is not yet required for its newly announced A13 technology.
Technology.source: This Week's Top Five Stories in Technology

Science.headline: Scientists sound alarm as dangerous amoebas spread globally
Science.detail: Free-living amoebae are emerging as a global health concern, fueled by warming temperatures and outdated water systems, with some species causing deadly infections and protecting other dangerous microbes. Their ability to survive heat and disinfectants makes them especially hard to control, prompting scientists to call for improved surveillance and water treatment.

Science.headline: 18th-century mechanical volcano roars to life 250 years later
Science.detail: Two University of Melbourne engineering students recreated a mechanical volcano first imagined in 1775 by volcanology enthusiast Sir William Hamilton. Using modern tools like LED lighting and electronic systems, they simulated the glowing flows and explosive drama of Mount Vesuvius, drawing from an 18th-century watercolor and a preserved sketch.

Science.headline: The National Academy of Sciences Elects 120 New Members For 2026
Science.detail: The National Academy of Sciences has elected 120 new members and 25 international members for 2026, recognizing scientists with distinguished and continuing achievements in original research. Election to the academy is widely regarded as one of the highest honors bestowed on a scientist.

Science.headline: German museum to return rare Irritator dinosaur skull to Brazil
Science.detail: A German museum is set to return a rare Irritator dinosaur skull to Brazil. This action highlights ongoing efforts to repatriate culturally and scientifically significant artifacts to their countries of origin.

Science.headline: First malaria drug for babies is approved in 'major public health milestone'
Science.detail: The approval of the first malaria drug for babies marks a significant milestone in public health. This development offers new hope for protecting infants in regions where malaria remains a major threat.

Health.headline: U.S. criticizes Zambia over delayed $1bn health aid deal
Health.detail: The U.S. government is criticizing Zambia over a delay in implementing a $1 billion health aid deal meant to fund key programs, including HIV treatment, malaria response, and maternal and child health; the U.S. claims a lack of engagement from Zambian officials since January has stalled progress.

Health.headline: Mushrooms can play crucial role in maintaining good health, Speakers say
Health.detail: Speakers at a regional mushroom fair in Khulna, Bangladesh, highlighted the nutritional and economic benefits of mushroom consumption and cultivation, noting their potential to support families and provide self-employment opportunities, especially for women and unemployed youth.

Health.headline: Young patients benefit from fundraising
Health.detail: Young patients in Walsall Children's Emergency Department have received toys thanks to Lloyds fundraisers.

Health.headline: An urgent care treated her allergic reaction. An ER monitored her
Health.detail: After being stung on her ankle while playing in a grass field, Silvana Toska received initial treatment for an allergic reaction at an urgent care facility, followed by monitoring at an emergency room.

Health.headline: FDA expands access to drug helping pancreatic cancer patients.
Health.detail: The FDA has expanded access to a drug that helps patients with pancreatic cancer.

Sports.headline: Barrett hits winning 3-pointer in OT as Raptors force Game 7, beat Cavaliers 112-110
Sports.detail: RJ Barrett hit a game-winning three-pointer in overtime to help the Toronto Raptors defeat the Cleveland Cavaliers 112-110, forcing a Game 7 in their playoff series.

Sports.headline: Goncalves scores in OT, Lightning beat Canadiens to force Game 7
Sports.detail: Anthony Goncalves scored in overtime as the Tampa Bay Lightning defeated the Montreal Canadiens to force a Game 7 in their series. The deciding game will be played in Tampa.

Sports.headline: Lakers' ruthless 27-3 surge buries Rockets 98-78 in Game 6, sets up Thunder series
Sports.detail: The Los Angeles Lakers used a dominant 27-3 run to defeat the Houston Rockets 98-78 in Game 6, setting up a playoff series against the Oklahoma City Thunder.

Sports.headline: India blank Chinese Taipei 3-0 riding on superb singles wins from Lakshya, Ayush
Sports.detail: India secured a 3-0 victory over Chinese Taipei in the Thomas Cup, propelled by outstanding singles performances from Lakshya Sen and Ayush Shetty.

Sports.headline: Alex Zanardi, auto racing champion who won Paralympic gold after life-altering accidents, dies at 59
Sports.detail: Alex Zanardi, a former Formula One driver and Paralympic gold medalist, has passed away at the age of 59. Zanardi was known for his resilience after suffering life-altering accidents that led him to excel in Paralympic sports.

Entertainment.headline: Missing Oscar found after director forced to check it on a flight out of JFK
Entertainment.detail: The Oscar statuette belonging to Pavel Talankin, the Russian director who won best documentary this year for “Mr. Nobody Against Putin,” has gone missing after he was forced to check the award into hold luggage on a flight from New York to Germany, his co-director said, but has since been found.

Entertainment.headline: Netflix to give Greta Gerwig's 'Narnia' wide theatrical release, marking a first.
Entertainment.detail: Netflix will give Greta Gerwig's 'Narnia' adaptation a wide theatrical release, marking the first time the streaming giant has done so for one of its major films.

Entertainment.headline: Gerry Conway, comic book writer who co-created Punisher character in Spider-Man, dies at 73.
Entertainment.detail: Gerry Conway, a comic book writer known for co-creating the Punisher character in Spider-Man, has passed away at the age of 73.

Entertainment.headline: Prasoon Joshi appointed chairman of Prasar Bharati
Entertainment.detail: Prasoon Joshi has been appointed as the chairman of Prasar Bharati, India's public broadcaster.

Entertainment.headline: Spotify's new 'Verified' badge confirms musicians like Taylor Swift, Drake aren't AI.
Entertainment.detail: Spotify has rolled out a new 'Verified by Spotify' badge to confirm that musicians like Taylor Swift and Drake are not AI-generated, aiming to distinguish real artists from AI creations.

Crime & Justice.headline: Man charged with attempted murder over stabbings of two Jewish men
Crime & Justice.detail: A 45-year-old man was charged Saturday with attempted murder in the stabbings of two Jewish men in London, in what police have labelled an act of terrorism. The suspect, Essa Suleiman, was remanded into custody and his case was transferred to the Central Criminal Court for a May 15 hearing.

Crime & Justice.headline: A Louisiana man exonerated for a crime was elected court clerk. His role was eliminated
Crime & Justice.detail: A man who spent 28 years in prison for a crime he did not commit was elected as clerk of court in New Orleans, but state lawmakers then eliminated his position.

Crime & Justice.headline: This Week in Fraud: DOJ's New Fraud Division Announces Numerous Fraud Enforcement Actions and a New Strike Force to Investigate and Prosecute Fraud on the West Coast
Crime & Justice.detail: The Department of Justice's new Fraud Division announced numerous fraud enforcement actions and a new strike force to investigate and prosecute fraud on the West Coast.

Crime & Justice.headline: Leader of Violent Trenton Street Gang Convicted of Murder, Racketeering and Other Crimes
Crime & Justice.detail: The leader of a violent Trenton street gang was convicted on charges including murder, attempted murder, aggravated assault, racketeering, and conspiracy, following a four-month trial, according to Attorney General Jennifer Davenport. Charles M. Willis, the leader of the “Get Money Boys” or “GMB,” was found guilty on 17 of 21 counts related to multiple shootings in Trenton.
Crime & Justice.source: New Jersey Office of Attorney General

Crime & Justice.headline: Dismissal sought in Michael Clark's second trial
Crime & Justice.detail: Defense seeks dismissal in the Michael Clark murder conviction due to a 32-year delay that led to crucial evidence being lost or destroyed, a bungled police investigation and “outrageous conduct” at the state crime lab. Clark was convicted of first-degree murder in October 2022 in a 1994 shooting death, but his original conviction was set aside after an independent retest of DNA evidence found differing results.

Education.headline: Odisha 10th Result Out: 95.33% Students Pass, Official Websites, Marksheet Download Link
Education.detail: The Odisha Class 10 results for 2026 have been released, with 95.33% of students passing; students can check their results on the official websites and download their marksheets.

Education.headline: Stop building schools for the poor. Start fixing education for all
Education.detail: The Jakarta Post argues that establishing separate schools for poor children doesn't break the cycle of poverty, but rather institutionalizes it, and Indonesia must commit to a single, high-quality education system. The article suggests that children are more likely to escape poverty when they experience excellent teaching, strong support, meaningful learning and real pathways into further study, employment and civic life.

Education.headline: NCERT Ramps Up Printing Of New Class 9 Textbooks On War Footing, Promises Quick Availability
Education.detail: NCERT is accelerating the printing of new Class 9 textbooks to ensure they are quickly available.

Education.headline: U.S. Department of Education Finalizes Landmark Rule to Lower College Costs and Simplify Student Loan Repayment
Education.detail: The U.S. Department of Education released a final rule that will lower the cost of college and make student loan repayment easier. This is an important step toward implementing reforms contained in President Trump's Working Families Tax Cuts Act.

Education.headline: Coimbatore school to host national conference on AI in education.
Education.detail: A school in Coimbatore is set to host a national conference focusing on the integration of Artificial Intelligence (AI) within the education sector.

Lifestyle.headline: The 5am myth: Getting up early won't always make you more successful
Lifestyle.detail: An article in The Independent discusses how waking up early isn't a guaranteed path to success, challenging the popular '5am club' mentality.

Lifestyle.headline: These daters are tired of apps – so they've turned to PowerPoints
Lifestyle.detail: The Independent reports on a new trend where people are using PowerPoint presentations to find love, moving away from traditional dating apps.

Lifestyle.headline: Meet the couple who quit banking and finance to run tours of Singapore's abandoned places
Lifestyle.detail: CNA Lifestyle features a story about a couple who left their careers in banking and finance to start a tour business showcasing Singapore's hidden and abandoned locations.

Lifestyle.headline: Paula Deen reveals the Kentucky Derby tradition she loves most — and it's not the food or drinks
Lifestyle.detail: Fox News covers Paula Deen's favorite Kentucky Derby tradition.

Lifestyle.headline: Skip the Dyson supersonic, I'd buy the new £250 travel dryer instead
Lifestyle.detail: The Independent features a review of a new travel hair dryer as an alternative to the Dyson supersonic.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: The Guardian view on Britain's fragile systems: when global shocks hit your shopping bill
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Global shocks are impacting shopping bills in Britain, highlighting the fragility of the country's systems.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: The Guardian view on the legacy of the Festival of Britain: look to the future
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The Festival of Britain's legacy encourages looking towards the future.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: How to Raise a Boy: As a single father, it was community that made sure I raised a well-rounded child.
Opinion/Editorial.detail: A single father discusses how community support was essential in raising his son to be well-rounded.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Spare a thought for the IPL bowler.
Opinion/Editorial.detail: An opinion piece reflecting on the challenges faced by bowlers in the Indian Premier League (IPL).

Opinion/Editorial.headline: The king went to Washington to save Britain's bacon.
Opinion/Editorial.detail: An analysis of King Charles's visit to the US and its potential implications for the UK.

World.headline: Zambia cancels world's largest human rights and tech summit days before start
World.detail: The Zambian government blocked the RightsCon 2026 conference, stating that it did not align with national values.

World.headline: Three killed in Israeli air strikes on southern Lebanese town
World.detail: Three people were killed in Israeli air strikes on a southern Lebanese town, as reported by Al Jazeera.

World.headline: Iran says it's 'likely' war with US to restart and it's 'fully prepared'
World.detail: According to Al Jazeera, Iran stated that war with the US is likely to restart and that they are fully prepared.

World.headline: King Charles urges U.S. to reject isolationism in landmark speech
World.detail: King Charles III addressed a joint session of Congress, advocating for the preservation of the alliance between the UK and the US, according to The Japan Times.

World.headline: Andaman sets Guinness World Record by unfurling largest underwater national flag
World.detail: The Andaman and Nicobar administration set a Guinness World Record by unfurling the world's largest underwater national flag at Radhanagar beach, measuring approximately 60 by 40 meters.

Zimbabwe.headline: Zimbabwe Embassy issues urgent safety alert amid rising SA xenophobic tensions
Zimbabwe.detail: The Zimbabwean Embassy in South Africa has issued an urgent safety advisory to Zimbabwean nationals, warning them to remain vigilant following rising xenophobic tensions and sporadic violence in parts of the neighbouring country. The alert comes in the wake of renewed anti-migrant protests, some of which have turned violent, with reports of planned demonstrations in the coming days worsening concern among foreign communities.

Zimbabwe.headline: Duo up for killing bar patron, injuring two others
Zimbabwe.detail: Two individuals are facing charges for allegedly killing a bar patron and injuring two others. No further details about the incident were provided in the search results.

Zimbabwe.headline: 'Zimbabwe's informal economy players are criminalised, harassed and forced into conflict with municipal police and ZRP' – representative bodies say
Zimbabwe.detail: Representative bodies are claiming that Zimbabwe's informal economy players are criminalised, harassed, and forced into conflict with municipal police and the ZRP.

Zimbabwe.headline: Police commend public for helping in fight against drugs and substance misuse
Zimbabwe.detail: The police are expressing their appreciation for the public's assistance in combating drug and substance abuse.

Zimbabwe.headline: Benjani Mwaruwari admits frustration over Highlanders' poor results
Zimbabwe.detail: Benjani Mwaruwari is reportedly feeling frustrated with the poor performance of the Highlanders. No details as to why he is frustrated were included in the search results.
